title: Piquant Venezuelan tofu curry
description: A fermented, creamy, comforting Venezuelan curry featuring mirin-hon and fermented kimchi.
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cube it.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat and add cumin and coriander, sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add cubed tofu to the skillet, stir to coat with spices, cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and stir in mirin-hon, coconut butter, and tamarind paste, bring to a gentle simmer.
Add chopped fermented kimchi, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes so flavors meld.
Season the curry with salt, stirring well, and adjust consistency with a splash of water if too thick.
Remove from heat, let the dish rest for 2 minutes so it thickens slightly.
Garnish with freshly chopped cilantro before serving.
